dialogueLines.speaker: Narrator
dialogueLines.text: School finally ends. Edward walks down the familiar cracked sidewalk, backpack heavier than it looks — weighed down by textbooks, deadlines, and responsibility.

dialogueLines.speaker: Narrator
dialogueLines.text: Being the eldest in his family means never having the luxury of giving up. His younger siblings look up to him. His mother depends on him.

dialogueLines.speaker: Edward (Barista)
dialogueLines.text: Mom’s probably exhausted again today… I need to do more. I have to step up.

dialogueLines.speaker: Narrator
dialogueLines.text: His part-time job cut his hours. Tuition fees circle him like sharks. Rent nips at his heels. But even under all that pressure, a small spark of hope pushes him forward.

dialogueLines.speaker: Edward (Barista)
dialogueLines.text: I’ll make this work. Somehow. I have to.

dialogueLines.speaker: Stage
dialogueLines.text: [A paper flutters loose on a lamppost ahead. Edward pauses.]

dialogueLines.speaker: Narrator
dialogueLines.text: The poster is old-fashioned — a hand-drawn logo of a teacup with tiny stars spiraling upward.

dialogueLines.speaker: Edward (Barista)
dialogueLines.text: The Wish House…? Never heard of it.

dialogueLines.speaker: Narrator
dialogueLines.text: He takes a slow breath. His legs ache from the long day, but his heart refuses to let him walk past this chance.

dialogueLines.speaker: Edward (Barista)
dialogueLines.text: If there’s even a small chance I can help Mom… I should try.

choices.prompt: What pushes Edward to take the poster?

options.text: “I need the money. My family needs me.”
“A new opportunity… maybe this is a sign.”
